title: Object 49
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Object 49: Wallpaper Marshmallows'''}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Roboto+Condensed:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&display=swap'); .thumbimage { border-radius: 50px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px black; } }} [[File:BowlofMarshmallows.jpg|center|frame]]
'''DESCRIPTION'''
'''Object 49''', hereby referred to as ''Wallpaper Marshmallows'', are foul-tasting sweets that take the appearance of an unassuming marshmallow and have a very distinct vanilla scent. Wallpaper Marshmallows have been given their name due to them tasting similar to their namesake and the fact that instances of Wallpaper Marshmallows are often found within metallic bowls behind a level's wallpaper. They are often a source of last-minute sustenance for lost wanderers deprived of food. Despite Wallpaper Marshmallows often being found within the deteriorating walls of most indoor levels, they are always pristine and lack any remnants from the wall or its insulation, regardless of whether or not debris from the damaged wall should land on the bowl or otherwise. This phenomenon is paired with the anomaly of bowls of Wallpaper Marshmallows only manifesting as soon as there is damage within a wallNot ''every'' wall, as the likelihood of Object 49 manifestation fluctuates between levels. that would lead to the bowl being visible. Contrary to their foul taste, Wallpaper Marshmallows are perfectly fine for human consumption in small amounts. Excessive consumption of Wallpaper Marshmallows can potentially cause damage to one's stomach. Furthermore, unnecessary consumption of Wallpaper Marshmallows should be avoided unless it is simply used as an ingredient in a larger meal. Wallpaper Marshmallows have an extremely filling effect regardless of whether or not one's stomach is actually full to the brim. This effect has led to Wallpaper Marshmallows being used as an additive within dying settlements to keep their members happy. Unrestrained consumption of Wallpaper Marshmallows can additionally lead to starvation, as one believes they are satiated and do not need to eat. Wallpaper Marshmallows found outside of a wall or large settlements should be avoided due to the possibility of them being laced with dangerous substances such as [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] or [[Object 44|Cashew Water]], this also applies to collections of Wallpaper Marshmallows outside of the usual metallic bowls they are normally found in. Additionally, one should avoid consumption of Wallpaper Marshmallows with a more pungent smell opposed to the usual sweet scent. Attempting to melt Wallpaper Marshmallows or providing unnecessary heat will lead to a chemical reaction within the marshmallow making consumption unsafe. This chemical reaction is irreversible, and affected Wallpaper Marshmallows can be identified with their discoloration into a yellow hue. While the change is irreversible, its effects can be negated. Gradually supplying melted Wallpaper Marshmallows with drops of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] can make affected instances relatively safe to consume. This technique is often used in settlements producing products using Wallpaper Marshmallows, though can be used if one accidentally applied heat to a bowl of marshmallows. Sweetened Wallpaper Marshmallows have been used in a plethora of recipes within the bases of [[M.E.G.]] and several other groups to keep morale high during problematic times. When low on supplies, several groups have been known to unnecessarily add Wallpaper Marshmallows to keep their members feeling full in order to avoid complaints and strain on the food supply. Wallpaper Marshmallows are occasionally found within the flooring of certain levels, usually drenched in [ DATA LOST ] or other substances that would make the affected instances unsafe for consumption. The appearance of Wallpaper Marshmallows within flooring is believed to be completely natural, as—like regular instances—there are no signs of damage besides the contamination from [ DATA LOST ].

'''APPLICATIONS'''
Wallpaper Marshmallows are usually reserved to being a quick yet disgusting snack whilst exploring or as an ingredient in a variety of recipes. As mentioned previously, Wallpaper Marshmallows are occasionally used as an additive by dying settlements attempting to keep their members full. Many groups utilize Wallpaper Marshmallows in M.R.E.'s during times of war, the filling effect of the marshmallows preventing soldiers from not preforming as well due to hunger pains.
